113‚Üí---
114‚Üí
115‚Üí## Backend Layers
116‚Üí
117‚Üí### Lucky5.Domain ‚Äî Core Engine (`Game/CleanRoom/`)
118‚Üí
119‚ÜíThis is the heart of the system. All files here are pure logic with no external dependencies.
120‚Üí
121‚Üí| File | Purpose |
122‚Üí|------|---------|
123‚Üí| `CoreModels.cs` | Enums and records: `HandCategory`, `PaytableProfile`, `RoundState`, card representations |
124‚Üí| `DeterministicRng.cs` | SplitMix64 seeded RNG ‚Äî all randomness flows through this |
125‚Üí| `FiveCardDrawEngine.cs` | Deal 5 cards, evaluate holds, draw replacements, evaluate final hand |
126‚Üí| `Lucky5DoubleUpEngine.cs` | Hi-Lo double-up loop: dealer card, guess, switch, 5‚ôÝ switch-only trigger, SafeFail, MachineClosed |
127‚Üí| `MachinePolicy.cs` | RTP management: distribution modes (COLD/NEUTRAL/HOT), deck alteration, payout scaling |
128‚Üí| `LineageProfiles.cs` | Cabinet variant profiles (Golden Poker, Bonus Poker lineage) |
129‚Üí| `PresentationNoiseGenerator.cs` | Separate noise seed for timing/visual variance |
130‚Üí

132‚Üí
133‚Üí| Entity | Purpose |
134‚Üí|--------|---------|
135‚Üí| `GameRound` | Single hand lifecycle: deal ‚Üí draw ‚Üí optional double-up ‚Üí settle. Tracks `WinAmount`, `OriginalWinAmount`, `IsPayoutSettled` |
136‚Üí| `MachineLedgerState` | Per-machine state: wallet balance, RTP tracking (CapitalIn/Out), jackpot values, streak counters, distribution mode |
137‚Üí| `User` | Player identity and authentication |
138‚Üí| `Machine` | Machine configuration |
139‚Üí| `PokerCard` | Card representation (suit + rank) |
140‚Üí
141‚Üí### Lucky5.Application ‚Äî Contracts & DTOs
142‚Üí
143‚ÜíService interfaces (`IGameService`, `IAuthService`) and all API data transfer objects. The DTOs define the exact shape of every API response.
144‚Üí
145‚ÜíKey DTOs:
146‚Üí- `DealResultDto` ‚Äî Cards dealt, round ID, jackpot state, advised holds
147‚Üí- `DrawResultDto` ‚Äî Final hand, win amount, hand rank, updated jackpots
148‚Üí- `DoubleUpResultDto` ‚Äî Guess result, current amount, wallet balance, status
149‚Üí
150‚Üí### Lucky5.Infrastructure ‚Äî Service Implementations

211‚Üí## Game Rules & Mechanics
212‚Üí
213‚Üí### Basic Flow
214‚Üí
215‚Üí1. **Bet** ‚Äî Player places bet (5,000‚Äì10,000, increment 100)
216‚Üí2. **Deal** ‚Äî 5 cards dealt; the machine session must already hold enough credits for both deal and draw
217‚Üí3. **Hold/Draw** ‚Äî Player selects holds (auto-hold suggests optimal); draw costs another bet
218‚Üí4. **Evaluate** ‚Äî Hand ranked, payout calculated per Lebanese paytable
219‚Üí5. **Win/Lose** ‚Äî If win, player can Take Score or enter Double-Up
220‚Üí6. **Double-Up** ‚Äî Optional Hi-Lo game to multiply winnings
221‚Üí
222‚Üí### Paytable (Lebanese Profile)
223‚Üí
224‚Üí| Hand | Multiplier |
225‚Üí|------|-----------|
226‚Üí| Royal Flush | 1000x |
227‚Üí| Straight Flush | 75x |
228‚Üí| Four of a Kind | 15x |
229‚Üí| Full House | 12x |
230‚Üí| Flush | 10x |
231‚Üí| Straight | 8x |
232‚Üí| Three of a Kind | 3x |
233‚Üí| Two Pair | 2x |
234‚Üí
235‚ÜíMinimum qualifying hand is **Two Pair**. Single pair does not pay.
236‚Üí
237‚Üí### Betting
238‚Üí
239‚Üí- Min: 5,000 / Max: 10,000 / Step: 100
240‚Üí- **Double deduction**: Deal costs 1x bet, Draw costs another 1x bet (total 2x per round)
241‚Üí- After completing a hand, first BET press resets to 5,000
242‚Üí- Starting credits: 200,000 (demo mode)
243‚Üí
244‚Üí### Auto-Hold
245‚Üí
246‚ÜíAfter dealing, the engine suggests optimal holds:
247‚Üí- Priority: quads > full house > flush > straight > trips > two pair > 4-to-flush > 4-to-straight > pair
248‚Üí- Player can override by clicking individual cards

251‚Üí
252‚Üí## Credit Accounting (Deferred Settlement)
253‚Üí
254‚ÜíThis is a critical architectural decision. Draw wins are **not** immediately credited to the player's wallet.
255‚Üí
256‚Üí### How It Works
257‚Üí
258‚Üí1. `DrawAsync` evaluates the hand and records `WinAmount` on the `GameRound` ‚Äî but does **not** add it to the wallet balance
259‚Üí2. The WIN indicator displays the pending win separately from CREDITS
260‚Üí3. Settlement (wallet credit) only occurs at:
261‚Üí   - **Take Score** ‚Äî Full cashout, triggers drain animation
262‚Üí   - **Take Half** ‚Äî Instant partial cashout (half to wallet, half continues in double-up)
263‚Üí   - **FinalizeDoubleUp** ‚Äî Double-up ends (win or lose)
264‚Üí
265‚Üí### Key Fields on GameRound
266‚Üí
267‚Üí| Field | Purpose |
268‚Üí|-------|---------|
269‚Üí| `WinAmount` | Current win value (may change via Take Half) |
270‚Üí| `OriginalWinAmount` | Immutable original draw win (for ledger delta tracking) |
271‚Üí| `IsPayoutSettled` | Idempotency guard ‚Äî prevents double-crediting on repeated cashout calls |
272‚Üí
273‚Üí### Why Deferred Settlement?
274‚Üí
275‚Üí- Base paytable wins stay gamble-eligible
276‚Üí- Jackpot wins go straight to machine credits and never enter double-up
277‚Üí- `SettledAmount` prevents partial-cashout accounting bugs
278‚Üí- `IsPayoutSettled` prevents double-cashout exploits
279‚Üí

282‚Üí## Progressive Jackpots
283‚Üí
284‚ÜíFour jackpot slots are tracked per machine:
285‚Üí
286‚Üí| Slot | Trigger Condition | Max Value | Reset Value (20%) |
287‚Üí|------|------------------|-----------|-------------------|
288‚Üí| Full House (FH) | FH trips rank matches player's chosen rank | 25,000,000 | 5,000,000 |
289‚Üí| Four of a Kind A (4K-A) | 4K hit while slot A is active | 999,999 | 200,000 |

297‚Üí- Machines can start at max jackpots

320‚Üí## Double-Up (Hi-Lo)
321‚Üí
322‚ÜíAfter any winning hand, the player can enter an inline Hi-Lo game to multiply their winnings.
323‚Üí
324‚Üí### Mechanics
325‚Üí
326‚Üí1. A **dealer card** is shown (face up)
327‚Üí2. Player guesses **BIG** (8‚ÄìK) or **SMALL** (2‚Äì6)
328‚Üí3. A **player card** is drawn:
329‚Üí   - Correct guess: win amount doubles
330‚Üí   - Wrong guess: lose everything
331‚Üí   - **Ace**: always wins regardless of guess direction
332‚Üí   - **7**: always loses (push/lose)
333‚Üí
334‚Üí### Switch Mechanic
335‚Üí
336‚Üí- Player can **switch** the dealer card up to **2 times** per round
337‚Üí- Each switch consumes the next card from the deterministic deck
338‚Üí- Strategic: allows avoiding unfavorable dealer cards
339‚Üí
340‚Üí### Five of Spades (5‚ôÝ) ‚Äî Lucky Card
341‚Üí
342‚ÜíWhen the **5 of Spades** appears as the dealer card (after a switch):
343‚Üí- Win amount is multiplied by **4x**
344‚Üí- **No-Lose mode** activates for that round
345‚Üí- If the player guesses wrong: **SafeFail** ‚Äî the current amount is banked instead of lost
346‚Üí- Visual: yellow flash, "LUCKY 5" banner
347‚Üí
348‚Üí### MachineClosed
349‚Üí
350‚ÜíIf machine credits reach or pass **50,000,000** through the final double-up jump:
351‚Üí- the current double-up result cashes out automatically into machine credits
352‚Üí- the machine is marked as closed for that player session
353‚Üí- the machine ledger itself does not reset
354‚Üí- player can cash out machine credits back to the lobby wallet subject to the machine-session rules
355‚Üí
356‚Üí### Take Score vs Take Half
357‚Üí
358‚Üí| Action | Effect |
359‚Üí|--------|--------|
360‚Üí| Take Score | Ends double-up, full amount drains to credits with animation |
361‚Üí| Take Half | Instantly takes half to credits (no animation), continues double-up with remaining half |
362‚Üí
363‚ÜíTake Half does **not** stop the double-up. If the remaining half is 0, it exits to idle.

367‚Üí## RTP & Machine Policy
368‚Üí
369‚ÜíTarget RTP: **87.5%** across all machines.
370‚Üí
371‚Üí### How RTP Is Achieved
372‚Üí
373‚ÜíThe natural base RTP from the paytable + double deduction is ~38%. The Machine Policy layer bridges this to 87.5% through two mechanisms:
374‚Üí
375‚Üí1. **Deck Composition Alteration** ‚Äî Before dealing, the policy can add or remove cards:
376‚Üí   - **COLD mode**: Removes high cards and 5‚ôÝ, making wins less likely
377‚Üí   - **NEUTRAL mode**: Standard deck
378‚Üí   - **HOT mode**: Injects extra 5‚ôÝ and high cards, making wins more likely
379‚Üí
380‚Üí2. **Payout Scaling** ‚Äî Win payouts are multiplied by a scale factor (~2.3x base):
381‚Üí   - Small wins (2P, 3K): ~0.96x of the current base scale
382‚Üí   - Medium wins (ST, FL, FH): ~1.02x of the current base scale
383‚Üí   - Big wins (4K, SF, RF): ~1.08x of the current base scale
384‚Üí
385‚ÜíThe current live policy also subtracts observed **jackpot RTP** from the base-game scaling target. That prevents the old failure mode where jackpots pushed the real RTP far above target while the scale logic still behaved as if only paytable wins existed.
386‚Üí
387‚Üí### Streak Management
388‚Üí
389‚ÜíThe policy tracks player win/loss streaks to create natural-feeling rhythm:
390‚Üí- `ConsecutiveLosses` counter biases toward HOT mode after 5+ losses
391‚Üí- After 15+ consecutive losses: forced HOT mode with maximum deck alteration
392‚Üí- After a win: 2‚Äì4 round cooldown before allowing next HOT phase
393‚Üí- Tracks `RoundsSinceMediumWin` for medium-tier pacing
394‚Üí
395‚Üí### Final QA note
396‚Üí
397‚Üí- jackpot wins now feed directly into machine credits and never enter double-up
398‚Üí- machine close is only evaluated on a real collecting event (cashout / final winning jump), not merely because a switched Lucky 5 preview amount looks large on screen
399‚Üí- extra Lucky 5 pressure in double-up now leans more on **5‚ôÝ** than on free ace-heavy decks, which keeps the feature visible without giving away as many automatic wins
400‚Üí

532‚Üí### Key Design Decisions
533‚Üí
534‚Üí1. **Clean-room approach**: Game logic was independently reimplemented based on behavioral analysis, not code copying
535‚Üí2. **Deterministic engine**: All randomness flows through SplitMix64 with known seeds ‚Äî every outcome is replayable
536‚Üí3. **Backend-authoritative**: Frontend is a thin display layer; all game state lives on the server
537‚Üí4. **Deferred settlement**: Wins are not credited until explicitly cashed out (prevents accounting bugs)
538‚Üí5. **Lebanese paytable**: Uses the specific multiplier profile from the original machines (Two Pair minimum, DEAL=red/BET=green convention)
539‚Üí
540‚Üí
541‚Üí## Machine Credit vs Wallet
542‚Üí
543‚Üí- Lobby balance is the player wallet.
544‚Üí- Game CREDIT is machine-session credit.
545‚Üí- Players cash in to the machine in **200,000** increments up to **1,000,000** total cash-in per session.
546‚Üí- Players can cash out machine credits back to the wallet when the machine is closed or when machine credits reach at least **2x** the session cash-in amount.
547‚Üí- Machine closed is only a gameplay event. It does **not** reset the machine ledger or RTP memory.
548‚Üí
549‚Üí## Variance Smoothing / Fun Policy
550‚Üí
551‚ÜíThe policy layer now also reacts to long Lucky 5 droughts, net-since-close pressure, and strongly counterintuitive player behavior. This lets the online version soften very long dry spells while still using the target RTP and machine-ledger driven policy loop. The intention is **not** to force unavoidable jackpots; counterplay pressure is only a soft variance smoother so players cannot reliably exploit strange play into guaranteed jackpot outcomes. Lucky 5 itself is now intentionally stricter: the no-lose mode only arms when 5‚ôÝ is reached through SWITCH, never from the opening dealer card and never from a revealed BIG/SMALL result card.
552‚Üí
553‚Üí## Admin Tooling
554‚Üí
555‚ÜíAdmin accounts can: 
556‚Üí
557‚Üí- credit or debit player wallets without resetting machines
558‚Üí- inspect machine RTP / net-since-close / streak / jackpot state
559‚Üí- inspect per-player machine sessions and current machine credits
560‚Üí- reset machine state separately when needed

5‚Üí## Live frontend
6‚Üí
7‚ÜíThe production frontend is the vanilla app served from:
8‚Üí
9‚Üí- `server/src/Lucky5.Api/wwwroot/index.html`
10‚Üí- `server/src/Lucky5.Api/wwwroot/js/game.js`
11‚Üí- `server/src/Lucky5.Api/wwwroot/css/game.css`
12‚Üí
13‚ÜíDo **not** start from the prototype folders for feature work unless you are intentionally rebuilding the frontend.
14‚Üí
15‚Üí## Core backend files
16‚Üí
17‚Üí- `server/src/Lucky5.Api/Controllers/GameController.cs` ‚Äî gameplay, machine session cash-in/out, jackpot rank, machine state
18‚Üí- `server/src/Lucky5.Api/Controllers/AuthController.cs` ‚Äî signup/login/OTP/profile/history
19‚Üí- `server/src/Lucky5.Api/Controllers/AdminController.cs` ‚Äî admin-only user/machine tools
20‚Üí- `server/src/Lucky5.Infrastructure/Services/GameService.cs` ‚Äî authoritative game flow
21‚Üí- `server/src/Lucky5.Infrastructure/Services/AdminService.cs` ‚Äî admin balance adjustments and machine inspection/reset
22‚Üí- `server/src/Lucky5.Infrastructure/Services/AuthService.cs` ‚Äî auth and wallet profile operations
23‚Üí- `server/src/Lucky5.Infrastructure/Services/InMemoryDataStore.cs` ‚Äî all in-memory state
24‚Üí- `server/src/Lucky5.Domain/Game/CleanRoom/MachinePolicy.cs` ‚Äî RTP / variance shaping / double-up deck pressure
25‚Üí
26‚Üí## Economy model
27‚Üí
28‚ÜíThere are now two balances:
29‚Üí
30‚Üí1. **Lobby wallet balance**
31‚Üí   - shown in the lobby / wallet screens
32‚Üí   - stored on `MemberProfile.WalletBalance`
33‚Üí2. **Machine credit balance**
34‚Üí   - shown on the game CREDIT display
35‚Üí   - stored per player + machine in `MachineSessionState.MachineCredits`
36‚Üí
37‚Üí### Machine session rules
38‚Üí
39‚Üí- players cash in to the machine in **200,000** increments
40‚Üí- total cash-in per machine session is capped at **1,000,000**
41‚Üí- cash-out to wallet is allowed when either:
42‚Üí  - the machine session is marked closed, or
43‚Üí  - machine credits reach at least **2x** total cash-in
44‚Üí
45‚Üí## Jackpot / double-up rules
46‚Üí
47‚Üí- jackpot values can start at max
48‚Üí- jackpot wins reset to 20% baseline values
49‚Üí- jackpot values never grow above their max caps
50‚Üí- jackpot win value goes straight to **machine credits**
51‚Üí- only the **base paytable win** enters double-up
52‚Üí- the Lucky 5 no-lose state only arms when 5‚ôÝ is reached through **SWITCH**; opening dealer 5‚ôÝ and BIG/SMALL reveal 5‚ôÝ are visual only
53‚Üí- the paytable row for the active hand is reused as the live score row during win/double-up
54‚Üí
55‚Üí## Machine closed
56‚Üí
57‚ÜíMachine closed is a gameplay event, not a hard reset.
58‚Üí
59‚Üí- machine ledgers continue accumulating history
60‚Üí- machine session is flagged closed for the current player
61‚Üí- player is expected to cash out machine credits back to the wallet
62‚Üí- machine RTP memory should keep running across closes
63‚Üí
64‚Üí## RTP / fun policy
65‚Üí
66‚Üí`MachinePolicy.cs` now uses:
67‚Üí
68‚Üí- target RTP
69‚Üí- jackpot RTP pressure (not just base paytable RTP)
70‚Üí- net since last close
71‚Üí- Lucky 5 drought
72‚Üí- loss streak pressure
73‚Üí- counterplay / counterintuitive-hold pressure
74‚Üí
75‚ÜíThis is meant to soften very long online dry spells while still letting the machine breathe with ups/downs and close misses.
76‚Üí
77‚ÜíThe latest tuning pass also tightened the negative correction when the observed RTP is already above target, so jackpot-heavy periods push the normal paytable scale down faster instead of compounding the overshoot.
78‚Üí
79‚Üí## Strange player behavior handling
80‚Üí
81‚Üí`GameService.DrawAsync()` compares actual holds against advised holds and updates `MachineSessionState.CounterplayScore`.
82‚Üí
83‚ÜíThis is then fed back into:
84‚Üí
85‚Üí- deal-time policy mode selection
86‚Üí- draw-time payout scale pressure
87‚Üí
88‚ÜíSo if a player repeatedly destroys strong hands or plays in an obviously counterintuitive way, the machine can compensate and keep the experience from becoming dead or overly punishing.
89‚Üí
90‚Üí## Admin tools
91‚Üí
92‚ÜíCurrent admin features:
93‚Üí
94‚Üí- search/list users
95‚Üí- credit or debit wallet balances with reasons
96‚Üí- inspect machine RTP and jackpot state
97‚Üí- inspect per-player machine sessions on each machine
98‚Üí- reset machine state separately from wallet adjustments
99‚Üí
100‚Üí## Adding a new lobby game
101‚Üí
102‚Üí1. Add a new entry to `AVAILABLE_GAMES` in `wwwroot/js/game.js`
103‚Üí2. Add any new route/screen handling in `showLobby()` / `openGame()`
104‚Üí3. Add the new backend controller/service if the game needs its own logic
105‚Üí4. Keep the existing Lucky5 frontend isolated so variants do not break the live cabinet
106‚Üí
107‚Üí## Known limitation / next important step
108‚Üí
109‚ÜíMachine ledgers and machine sessions still live in memory. That means they can survive for long-running single-instance deployments, but **not** across process restarts or redeploys.
110‚Üí
111‚ÜíAlso note that `DealAsync()` now requires enough machine credits for **both** deal and draw up front. That is deliberate: it avoids dead-end rounds where a player can start a hand but cannot afford the mandatory draw cost.
112‚Üí
113‚ÜíFor true month-scale durability, the next big engineering step is persistent storage for:
114‚Üí
115‚Üí- `MachineLedgerState`
116‚Üí- `MachineSessionState`
117‚Üí- optionally audit/history rows
118‚Üí
EOF
